rationale: The citing paper discusses Guo (2015) in detail as a key prior model: it summarizes that paper's setup (cost uncertainty, price-based inference, exogenous quality) and explicitly positions its own model as a complement. The 2016 paper repeatedly contrasts its results with Guo (2015), including opposite comparative statics for firm profit under stronger inequity aversion, and attributes the difference to adding endogenous quality choice. This indicates the citation is used for core framing and theory development, not a background list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Guo (2015) by moving from a pricing-only signaling model with exogenous quality to a joint price-and-quality signaling framework under consumer fairness concerns. It builds on that baseline to derive new separating/pooling outcomes and different welfare and profit implications.
